task_completion.description: Verify the tiddler is created with the correct title and tags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the TiddlyWiki edit view or rendered view, the title must be exactly 'Troubleshooting: Internet Connectivity' and the tags must include 'Troubleshooting' and 'NetworkSupport'.

task_completion.description: Verify the guide begins with the correct starting question and Yes/No options.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tiddler content (either in edit source or rendered view) must show the text 'Can the user access any websites?' accompanied by 'Yes' and 'No' buttons.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'No' branch contains the correct gateway and ping check logic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tiddler source code or rendered view must contain logic/text for a gateway ping check. It must advise physical/cable checks if unreachable, and check ping to 8.8.8.8 if reachable (distinguishing between DNS resolution and routing/firewall issues).

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Yes' branch contains the correct partial internet logic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tiddler source code or rendered view must contain logic/text distinguishing between slow speeds (advising a speed test) and specific site issues (advising a proxy/firewall check).

task_completion.description: Verify the use of <$reveal> widgets, <$button> widgets, and the correct state tiddler.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tiddler source code in edit mode must explicitly show the use of '<$reveal>' and '<$button>' tags, and contain references to the state tiddler '$:/state/troubleshoot-internet'.

task_completion.description: Verify the presence of 'Start Over' buttons at the resolution steps.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tiddler source code or rendered view must include 'Start Over' buttons designed to reset the state tiddler back to the beginning of the flowchart.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the TiddlyWiki web interface to create the tiddler.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the TiddlyWiki application in a web browser, specifically interacting with the 'New Tiddler' or edit tiddler interface.

integrity.description: Verify the agent wrote actual TiddlyWiki widget syntax rather than plain text mimicking a flowchart.
integrity.visual_evidence: The edit mode screenshot should show valid TiddlyWiki syntax (e.g., <$button>, <$reveal>) rather than just static text like '-> If No, do this'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal commands to inject the tiddler directly into the file system or server.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of terminal windows using commands like 'curl', 'echo', or 'node' to bypass the TiddlyWiki GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected title is exactly 'Troubleshooting: Internet Connectivity'. The expected tags are 'Troubleshooting' and 'NetworkSupport'. The state tiddler used to manage the flowchart state must be exactly '$:/state/troubleshoot-internet'.
